hero.question: Will Cleveland Guardians win the 2026 American League Championship Series?
hero.prob_take: At 5.3%, the market is saying the Guardians have essentially one realistic path to the ALCS: a perfect storm of health, bullpen performance, and playoff luck that rarely happens in October baseball. That's probably right, given their current roster construction and division competition, but don't sleep on young core development and the fact that 5.3% still implies roughly 1-in-19 odds, closer to fair value than truly mispriced.

daily_take.headline: Guardians ALCS odds cratered 450bps. Check if the market panicked or saw something real.
daily_take.deck: Cleveland's 2026 pennant chances dropped from 9.8% to 5.3% in 24 hours on $342K volume, a brutal repricing. The move looks thin, reactive, and detached from actual roster construction or the AL landscape. Smart money doesn't dump a playoff contender this hard without new injury news or trade fallout. The floor is probably north of 7%, which means backing the Guardians here extracts value from an overcorrected market.
